frame_label: Sindoor, Mangalsutra, Shankha-Pola, Name Change: Looking Married
generated_comment: Nothing wrong in wearing mangalsutra or taking husband’s name after marriage. It is not about control, it is about respect, family identity and our tradition. Nowadays some women say it is old-fashioned, but honestly a married woman looks more settled when she carries those signs proudly.
label: misogynistic
annotation_note: This is covert misogyny because it frames visible marital markers and name change as natural, respectful, and identity-giving for women, while judging women who reject them as less proper. It encodes the surface manifestations of completeness framing, tradition framing, and disapproval of non-wearing.
original_frame: Other Man's Ownership Over Woman's Own Consent.
persona_text: You are Harish. Age 31. Single. A man in Chennai. You are posting anonymously on a Tamil Facebook group after a video went viral of a young woman being stopped by her father and brothers at a bus stand and forcibly taken home because she was trying to leave with her boyfriend. Most comments are supporting the father. You are adding your comment and explaining why you think the father was right and what would have happened if he had not intervened and what daughters owe their families.
